Role type: Full-time; Continuing
Department/ School: Enterprise Services Group
Salary: UOM 9 $146,049 - $151,951 p.a. plus 17% super

Design and optimise scalable data pipelines / Collaborate on cutting-edge analytics projects / Enhance data security and governance
Apply now to take ownership of critical data infrastructure, mentor the next generation of data engineers, and shape the technical direction of analytics and ML capabilities serving the entire University community.
Investing in you - benefits package including salary packaging, health and wellbeing programs, discounted services, and professional development opportunities.

The University of Melbourne
We take pride in our people, who all contribute to our mission to benefit society through the transformative impact of education and research. Discover more via our website.

Your next career opportunity
As a Senior Data Engineer, you will have the opportunity to design, build, and maintain robust data pipelines within the University's Enterprise Data Lake. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to support advanced analytics and machine learning, ensuring seamless data integration and high performance. Your expertise will be pivotal in driving data governance and security, contributing to a transformative data environment.

Your responsibilities will include:

Architect, built and own production data pipelines supporting University wide analytics and reporting, ensuring reliability, performance and data quality.
Lead technical design for data integration projects, translating business requirements into scalable solutions with full delivery accountability.
Mentor and develop data engineering members, establishing coding standards, conducting design reviews, fostering excellence.
Define and evolve data engineering best practices, evaluating emerging technologies and influencing technical direction.

You may be a great fit if:
You are an experienced data engineer with deep expertise in Snowflake and dbt, a track record of leading and mentoring engineering teams, and solid experience with AWS technologies, adept at leading pipeline development and optimising data storage and retrieval, while collaborating effectively in cross-functional teams.

You may also:

Have 10 or more years of experience in data engineering, particularly with Snowflake and dbt in production environments
Demonstrate deep hands-on experience with Snowflake (architecture, performance tuning, data modelling) and dbt (modelling, testing, documentation, CI/CD integration).
Possess strong proficiency in Python and SQL for data processing and transformation.
Strong experience with AWS services (S3, Glue, Lambda, CDK) and CI/CD pipelines.
Exhibit excellent problem-solving skills for optimising data pipeline performance.
Be skilled in communicating complex data engineering concepts to diverse stakeholders.

For further information, please refer to the attached PD.

What we offer you!
We offer the opportunity to be part of a vibrant community and enjoy a comprehensive range of benefits to support your success and sense of fulfilment, including:

Supportive flexible work arrangements underpinned by our commitment to inclusion and well-being .
Progressive, considerate leave provisions to empower your work-life balance
Salary packaging and access to a range of discounted services including Bupa health insurance
Health and well-being services including a leading Employee Assistance Program

For more information check out our benefits page!

Your new team - Enterprise Services Group
Enterprise Services Group delivers functions that facilitate the design, evaluation, and improvement of services across the University, and business partnering services to Chancellery. The ESG brings together a range of cognate business functions and expertise such as Data Governance, Management and Analytics; Business Transformation and Decision Support; Operational and Service Improvement, Service Design, Experience, Culture and Capabilities; Digital Operations; and Business Continuity Planning.

Why Smart?

Look forward to working with Australia's leading and most trusted provider of Employee Management Services, making the benefits of salary packaging, novated leasing, and fleet management readily available to employers and their employees.

Working at Smartgroup offers more than a job. It is a dynamic workplace that matches the enthusiasm for the work you do and ambition for an exciting fast-paced career. We have all been hired for a purpose and a reason.

Every day you will be part of a team that helps support fellow Australians - many working in essential services, corporates, government, and not-for-profits - to take home more money in their pay packets.

About the Data Engineer role:


This is an exciting opportunity for a Data Engineer to work at the cutting edge of modern data platforms and applied AI. Sitting within a high-performing Data & AI squad, you'll play a key role in building scalable, cloud-based data solutions that power real business outcomes. From developing robust data pipelines to contributing to next-generation AI and Generative AI use cases, this role offers genuine variety, impact, and the chance to work on meaningful, forward-thinking technology initiatives.

Key Responsibilities:

Design and deliver scalable, high-performance data pipelines and platforms using Azure and Databricks
Build and optimise ELT/ETL workflows leveraging Spark (PySpark) and SQL
Develop reliable batch and streaming data solutions to support business-critical analytics and AI use cases
Contribute to the design and development of AI and Generative AI solutions, including LLM-powered applications
Implement best practices across data modelling, quality, governance, and architecture
Develop data pipelines supporting GenAI use cases (e.g. embeddings, vector data, LLM outputs)
Collaborate closely with data scientists, ML engineers, and stakeholders to deliver impactful, production-ready solutions

Skills & Experience:

Solid experience as a Data Engineer in a cloud environment, ideally within Microsoft Azure
Strong hands-on capability with Databricks, Spark (PySpark) and SQL
Proven ability to design and optimise data pipelines and data models
Understanding of CI/CD, version control, and testing practices within data environments
Exposure to AI/LLM-based solutions or advanced analytics use cases is highly regarded
Familiarity with Azure services (e.g. Data Factory, DevOps) and an interest in MLOps/LLMOps

What's in it for you?

Smart team members enjoy a range of benefits designed to support flexibility, wellbeing, reward, and growth:

Smart Leave, an additional week of leave each year
Free lunch every week
Up to 20 weeks Paid Parental Leave with superannuation contributions and a Return-to-Work Bonus
Make a Difference Days - paid days off to volunteer for the community and environment
Discounted retail vouchers
Salary packaging and novated leasing benefits (of course!)
Free flu vaccinations
Employee Share Plan

Grow with Purpose

At Smart, we're driven by purpose and performance. We support individual and team growth via varied learning opportunities, open communication, and leaders who work to get the basics right - quality and regular one to ones, clear goals and success metrics, and growth conversations.

Show us you have what it takes

If this sounds like the opportunity for you and you have something to offer, we'd love to hear from you. APPLY ONLINE today.

Our Talent Acquisition team will be in touch to discuss next steps. If you require any adjustments to the recruitment process or are seeking part-time work, let us know—we're here to understand.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

Everyone belongs and is welcome at Smart. We welcome people from diverse backgrounds to join our team, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, veterans, people with disability, people who identify as LGBTQ+, all gender identities, and people of any socioeconomic status, age, race, national origin or ethnicity.

Our commitment to Diversity & Inclusion is reflected in the external recognition we've received, including being named:

WGEA Employer of Choice for Gender Equality since 2019
DCA Inclusive Employer 2025 - 2026
Bronze Employer on the Australian Workplace Equality Index

Respectfully, no agencies please.

Optus Network Engineering keeps Australia connected. We own the design, planning, delivery, and operation of one of Australia's largest and most critical telecommunications networks, keeping 11 million customers online every day. We support the IP infrastructure that powers mobile, broadband, and digital services across the nation, and we're looking for a Solutions Architect who can shape how that infrastructure is automated, integrated, and evolved at national scale.

The IP Automation & Tooling team develops and operates automation, observability, and data platforms spanning IP Core, Edge, Aggregation, and Optical domains. We own the end-to-end roadmap for transforming our IP network into an intelligent, self-healing platform. That means integrating across a complex landscape of vendor systems, cloud platforms, and internal OSS/BSS, and making deliberate architectural decisions about how those pieces fit together. The Solutions Architect is the person who leads that thinking.

You'll join a diverse team of network engineers, software developers, and data scientists tackling some of telco's most complex integration and automation challenges. You'll have the autonomy to set architectural direction, evaluate technology, and lead the design of solutions that operate at national scale, with the trust of the team to own those decisions end to end.

What You'll Do

Lead end-to-end solution architecture across the automation and observability platform, from initial problem framing through to design sign-off and delivery oversight.
Define how our platform integrates with vendor systems (Cisco, Nokia, Juniper), cloud providers, and internal OSS/BSS, establishing API integration patterns and standards that scale across the platform.
Evaluate emerging technologies, run proof-of-concept initiatives, and make clear recommendations on technology selection, vendor capability, and build vs. buy decisions.
Translate complex network operational requirements into coherent, maintainable platform architectures, ensuring solutions address performance, reliability, security, and operational coverage.
Work across Optus teams and external partners to align architectural decisions with business strategy, network roadmaps, and engineering standards (TM Forum, TOGAF, IETF).
Provide architectural governance: review designs from the engineering team, set integration standards, and ensure consistency across the platform.
Mentor engineers on architecture practice and platform thinking, and represent the team in cross-functional design forums and vendor engagements.

What You Bring

Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.
4+ years in solutions architecture, enterprise architecture, or senior platform engineering, with meaningful experience in a telco, network, or large-scale infrastructure environment.
Strong grasp of telco system integration, including OSS/BSS platforms, network management systems, and how vendor products connect into operational workflows via RESTful and event-driven APIs.
Solid understanding of software architecture principles: API design, microservices, service decomposition, and how to structure systems that are maintainable at scale.
Familiarity with network automation concepts and southbound integration with network devices (NETCONF, gNMI, model-driven programmability) sufficient to evaluate and govern solutions without implementing them directly.
Experience evaluating and integrating cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) into network or operational technology stacks.
Working knowledge of IP networking: routing protocols (BGP, IS-IS, OSPF), MPLS, segment routing, and transport network design.
Exposure to AI/ML in operational contexts (intent-based automation, anomaly detection, closed-loop assurance) and an ability to assess where it adds genuine value.
Architecture standards experience: TOGAF, TM Forum eTOM/Open APIs, or equivalent.
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills; able to present architectural trade-offs clearly to engineering teams, senior leadership, and external vendors alike.

What's in it for you?

Competitive remuneration and employee discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and unique shopping discounts with our retail partners.
Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.
We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.
Direct your own development and build future-ready skills with Optus U's industry-focused micro-credentials, developed with La Trobe and Macquarie Universities.
Build meaningful connections through employee-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).
Put your wellbeing first with free access to counselling and support services, 24/7 — in-person, by phone, SMS, or video.

At Optus, we are strengthened by others and that means valuing diversity and saying ‘yes' to embracing individual differences. We are committed to ensuring that our application process provides an equal employment opportunity to all job seekers, including individuals from diverse gender, cultural and linguistic backgrounds, individuals with a disability, individuals identifying as being part of the LGBTQIA+ community, individuals who may have served in the armed forces or who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. We also want to do our best to make our recruitment process inclusive. If you require any adjustments or accessibility support to participate fairly and equitably in the recruitment process, please email AccessibleRecruitment@optus.com.au or call 18•• ••• 170. We're here to help.

For more information on Diversity, Inclusion & Belonging at Optus, please visit https://www.optus.com.au/about/inclusion-diversity

AI Engineer – Shape the Future of Heritage Consulting at Artefact

We have an exciting opportunity for an AI Engineer to join Artefact Heritage & Environment a leading, purpose-led heritage consultancy known for its collaborative culture, commitment to innovation, and genuine passion for cultural heritage outcomes.

Based at our head office on a heritage wharf overlooking Sydney Harbour (Pyrmont), this role offers the chance to make a real and lasting impact across a multidisciplinary team of archaeologists, historians, heritage consultants, environmental specialists, and project managers while working in one of the most distinctive office settings in Sydney.

Artefact is establishing a structured AI function to support staff through smarter workflows, improved productivity, reduced project administration burden, and clearer, more consistent transparency in how we manage and deliver our heritage services. This role sits at the centre of that vision.

We are seeking a mid–senior AI Engineer / AI Solutions Lead to take ownership of AI solution design and delivery across the business. This is a rare opportunity to build and shape AI capability from the ground up, forming the foundation of Artefact's long-term AI strategy.

This is a hands-on, delivery-focused role, suited to someone who enjoys working closely with diverse business teams to solve real operational challenges and scale practical, high-impact solutions, and who is excited by the opportunity to apply AI within the unique context of the heritage and environmental consulting sector.

In your application, please indicate your salary expectation.

In this role, you will:

Design and implement AI solutions that drive measurable productivity improvements across our project delivery and support functions

Work closely with team leaders, project managers, and field consultants to identify inefficiencies and deliver practical outcomes

Build, test, and scale AI tools and workflows across the organisation, from report automation to client transparency dashboards

Contribute to how AI is embedded, governed, and adopted responsibly across the business, with particular sensitivity to cultural data, Indigenous knowledge, and ethical AI use

Support the development of AI-enabled client reporting and communication tools

As Artefact continues to grow across NSW and beyond, so will this role - offering a clear pathway from hands-on delivery to owning the AI roadmap, leading initiatives, and influencing strategic decision-making.

Why this opportunity stands out

Build something meaningful from the ground up – play a key role in shaping Artefact's AI capability within a purpose-driven organisation

Real and unique impact – your work will directly support operations and heritage project outcomes for government, corporate, and community clients

A values-led environment – Artefact is a purpose-led organisation where social and environmental justice are embedded in our culture

Distinctive workplace – work from a heritage wharf overlooking Sydney Harbour, with flexible work options available

Practical innovation – apply AI to genuine business challenges in a sector that is only beginning to explore what AI can do

This is an ideal opportunity for someone looking for a role with ownership, visibility, and genuine impact, within a growing business that is investing seriously in technology and its people.

Who we are

Artefact Heritage & Environment is a leading heritage consultancy firm in NSW, providing archaeological, cultural heritage management, environmental assessment, and heritage interpretation services. We work with government, corporate, and community clients, from large-scale infrastructure projects to targeted urban developments.

We operate from our head office at Wharf 10, Pyrmont, and regional offices in Bathurst and Newcastle. Our diverse team of heritage professionals, archaeologists, historians, and environmental specialists is known for personalised service, strong stakeholder relationships, particularly with Aboriginal communities and Land Councils, and a deeply collaborative approach to project delivery.

As a purpose-led organisation, social and environmental justice is in our DNA. We encourage like-minded people to join us and follow our Purpose: to use Artefact as an agent for positive change.

Our vision is to illuminate the past and inspire the future, and we hope you'll join us and play a part in achieving that goal.

Responsibilities

AI solution design and delivery

Design and develop AI-enabled solutions that support teams, improve productivity, and reduce manual administration across project delivery workflows

Build, test, and refine prompts and workflows for tools such as Microsoft Copilot and Power Platform

Assess existing platforms and automation tools before proposing custom solutions

Develop prototypes and pilot initiatives, then support broader rollout across the organisation

Work across platforms including Microsoft 365, Power Platform, SharePoint,  BOX, Total Synergy, Xero, Prospend and other operational systems to improve workflow automation and business visibility

Explore and implement practical AI capabilities including LLM integration, document intelligence, workflow orchestration, and API-based automation solutions where appropriate

Business engagement

Partner with team leaders and project managers across all business units to identify inefficiencies and improvement opportunities

Analyse workflows, systems, and data flows across project delivery, reporting, and support functions

Propose practical, scalable solutions aligned with business priorities and Artefact's purpose commitments

Implementation and scaling

Support deployment of AI tools and workflows across multidisciplinary teams

Assist with integration into existing systems where appropriate

Define and track success metrics such as time saved, accuracy, adoption rates, and operational impact

Change and capability uplift

Support training and adoption of AI tools across technical and non-technical staff

Develop guidance, templates, and best-practice standards for responsible AI usage

Build confidence and AI literacy across the organisation

Drive measurable operational improvements including reduced administrative workload, improved reporting consistency, and better knowledge accessibility across the organisation

Client transparency initiatives

Contribute to AI-enabled client reporting, project dashboards, and automated project updates

Improve consistency and visibility of project information across the client lifecycle

Governance and responsible AI

Work within governance frameworks covering data privacy, security, and AI risk — with particular attention to the ethical use of AI in contexts involving Aboriginal cultural heritage data and Indigenous Cultural and Intellectual Property (ICIP)

Support compliant, ethical, and culturally sensitive use of AI aligned with Artefact's organisational values and risk appetite

Growth opportunity
This role is designed to grow with the organisation. As AI adoption scales, responsibilities may expand to include:

Ownership of Artefact's AI roadmap and prioritisation framework

Leadership of AI adoption initiatives across business units

Oversight of AI governance, standards, and ethical frameworks

First 6–12 month priorities

Identify and prioritise high-impact operational inefficiencies across project delivery and support functions

Support sustainable and responsible AI adoption practices across the organisation

Improve reporting transparency and workflow consistency across teams

What we're looking for

Experience

Experience in applied AI, prompt engineering, automation, or AI-enabled solution design

Hands-on exposure to Microsoft 365, Microsoft Copilot, Copilot Studio, Power Platform, Power Automate, Azure AI, or similar productivity-focused AI and automation environments

Experience working across business functions to design and implement operational solutions

Coding and/or software development experience valued

Experience with Python, Azure AI, OpenAI, Claude, API integrations, workflow orchestration, document intelligence, process automation, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), or similar AI development and automation environments highly regarded.

Experience in consulting, professional services, workflow transformation, or internal business enablement environments highly regarded

Capabilities

Strong ability to translate complex business problems into clear, practical solutions

Comfortable prototyping, testing, and iterating in live environments

Strong communication skills with the ability to explain AI concepts clearly to non-technical stakeholders

Analytical mindset with experience mapping workflows and identifying inefficiencies

Mindset

Practical and outcome-oriented

Curious and adaptable, comfortable operating in a growing and evolving AI environment

Strong sense of responsibility around data security, ethical AI use, and cultural sensitivity

Collaborative and credible when working with senior stakeholders and specialist technical teams alike

An appreciation for, or genuine interest in, the heritage and environmental sectors is highly regarded

Comfortable operating in a greenfield environment with high level of ownership, autonomy, and visibility across the organisation

Why this role is different

This is not a traditional IT support or infrastructure role — it is focused on applied AI solutions that support teams, streamline workflows, improve operational visibility, and enhance business processes

Opportunity to help shape Artefact’s long-term AI roadmap and capability from the ground up

Work within a complex operational environment involving large volumes of unstructered project information, stakeholder coordination, regulatory workflows, and knowledge management challenges

Employee Benefits

Professional development leave and allowances

Flexible work options (hybrid, Pyrmont-based)

Allocated time to work on social and environmental justice initiatives through our Purpose Team program

Clear career pathways and industry-leading personal wellbeing support

Employee assistance program

Annual performance appraisal and learning & development plans

Competitive salary package

Regular team activities in a supportive, multidisciplinary environment

Artefact Heritage & Environment highly values diversity and is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applications from all suitably qualified candidates, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples.

This role is open to Australian Citizens and Permanent Residents only (including New Zealand Citizens).

If this exciting opportunity has captured your interest, please submit your application via the apply button or contact us at hr•@artefact.net.au for a confidential discussion.

Artefact acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of the lands on which we work — the Gadigal, Wangal, Worimi, Wonnarua and Awabakal peoples — and pays respect to Elders past, present, and emerging.

About QTAC:

Queensland Tertiary Admissions Centre (QTAC) is a not-for-profit organisation that provides undergraduate and postgraduate tertiary entry and application services for 17 major universities and tertiary education institutions in Queensland and northern New South Wales. QTAC also calculates and releases the Queensland ATAR for year 12 students.

QTAC is here to help match tertiary applicants with an opportunity that is right for them. Offering an equitable centralised tertiary entrance system, QTAC provides comprehensive information and support for prospective applicants, receiving and processing applications and making offers across the calendar year.

QTAC’s mission is to connect people with organisations that offer professional and personal learning opportunities, helping them thrive in an ever-changing environment.

About the role

As a Data Engineer within QTAC’s Data & Analytics team, you will play a key role in designing, developing and maintaining scalable data integration and transformation solutions that support critical business operations and decision-making. Working across modern cloud and hybrid environments, you’ll help drive the evolution of QTAC’s data platform, including supporting the migration of legacy systems to Microsoft Fabric.

This is an exciting opportunity for a technically strong and solutions-focused professional who enjoys working with complex data environments, continuous improvement, and delivering high-quality data solutions that make a real impact.

Key Responsibilities

Design, develop and maintain scalable data integration and transformation pipelines

Support the ongoing development and optimisation of QTAC’s data warehouse and cloud data platform

Build and maintain Python-based ingestion and transformation pipelines

Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business requirements and deliver data solutions

Develop and maintain data models, interfaces and automated integration processes

Support the migration of legacy data pipelines to Microsoft Fabric and modern cloud technologies

Troubleshoot, maintain and improve existing data pipelines and platform performance

Ensure data quality, consistency and reliability through testing and validation processes

Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives across data engineering practices and deployment methods

Prepare and maintain technical documentation and support materials

Work collaboratively with the Data & Analytics team to deliver governed and reliable data products

Identify opportunities to improve how data is surfaced, accessed and used across the organisation


Skills & Experience

Proven experience in data engineering, including developing and maintaining data pipelines and ETL/ELT solutions

Strong SQL skills, including performance tuning, stored procedures and database optimisation

Hands-on experience with data modelling methodologies such as Data Vault 2.0 or Kimball dimensional modelling

Experience working with relational and non-relational data sources, APIs and flat files

Hands-on experience with Python and/or SQL-based data transformation tools

Experience working within cloud and hybrid data environments

Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to troubleshoot and optimise data solutions

Ability to engage with stakeholders and translate business requirements into technical solutions

Understanding of data quality, testing and validation practices

Familiarity with Git-based version control and CI/CD pipelines for deploying data assets

Experience with Microsoft Fabric, dbt, Power BI or modern data platforms will be highly regarded

Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Engineering or a related field preferred

Benefits

Competitive salary ($130,000 – $140,000 + super)

Supportive and inclusive workplace culture

Opportunities for professional growth and development

Convenient Milton location, close to public transport


How to Apply

If this role sounds like you, we would love to hear from you.
Please submit your application, including your resume and a cover letter, via SEEK.

This is a permanent position based in Milton, Qld with a Hybrid Work from Home and Office arrangement. 

Please note, you must have full rights to work in Australia to be considered for this role.

About the role

At Saab Australia, we design, integrate and sustain advanced defence systems that support Australia's sovereign capability and national security.

We're seeking a Modelling & Analysis Engineer to contribute to the development and performance evaluation of advanced integrated Combat Systems.

This is a role where mathematical rigour meets practical application-ideal for someone who thrives on solving complex, high-stakes engineering problems and wants their work to directly inform capability decisions.

We work in a fast-paced, demanding, challenging as well as time sensitive environment; we're diverse, global, resilient, adaptable and agile, therefore the ability to solve problems is critical.

Along with satisfaction & rewards that comes with working for a global defence company, you can expect a generous salary, a range of outstanding benefits & an inclusive working environment.

This position also offers Flexible working options and a 9 Day Fortnight

The Opportunity

As a key member of a multidisciplinary engineering team, you will lead and contribute to software modelling, simulation, and analytical studies that support system design, integration, and operational effectiveness.

You'll work across the full modelling lifecycle-from concept and development through to experimentation, validation, and performance analysis-ensuring robust, defensible insights are delivered to stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities:

Develop, enhance and maintain mathematical and software-based models to support system analysis and trade studies
Integrate and validate models within broader system environments
Design and execute structured experiments to evaluate system and algorithm performance across multiple metrics
Perform statistical analysis on simulation and trial data to generate meaningful insights
Produce high-quality technical documentation, including algorithms, experimental designs, and software artefacts
Contribute to system-level understanding, including operational context and real-world application
Support internal and external reviews, ensuring outputs align with engineering and quality processes
Communicate progress, risks, and technical challenges clearly to team leadership
Collaborate effectively with cross-functional stakeholders across engineering disciplines

About You

This role is suitable for an engineer who enjoys turning complex systems into measurable, testable insights-and influencing real-world defence capability.

Skills & Experience:

5+ years' experience in software engineering, systems engineering, or mathematical modelling
Strong programming capability in one or more of: Python, MATLAB, C/C++, or Java
Proven experience solving complex, multi-variable engineering problems within a collaborative team environment
Ability to quickly grasp and apply advanced technical concepts independently
Experience designing experiments and analysing data using statistical methods
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present technical findings clearly
A mindset geared toward innovation, curiosity, and continuous improvement


As Defence security clearance is required for this role, applicants must be Australian citizens and eligible to obtain and maintain an NV1 clearance.

If you're looking for a role where yours modelling and analytical skills directly shape complex system performance-and you enjoy working at the intersection of theory, simulation, and real-world application-this is a strong opportunity to step into.

About us

Saab Australia is a defence, security and cyber solutions provider, specialising in the development and integration of major defence and security systems. For over 30 years in Australia, we have built a reputation for delivering complex systems integration projects that provide proven and advanced capabilities to keep us ahead of today's challenges.

From combat, communications, security and underwater systems to mixed reality, cyber security and deployable health solutions, our extensive portfolio of products and services provide high-technology solutions for defence forces and civil industries across the globe.

Apply today to join our crew and deliver solutions with purpose.

Culture and benefits

We employ over 1000 Australians and are committed to fostering a culture of excellence and enriching the employee experience.

By joining Saab, you'll be a part of a collaborative and supportive organisation, where your professional growth and work-life balance are emphasised. You'll work within an innovative and vibrant workplace, while having the opportunity to develop a long-term, fulfilling career.

As a valued team member of Saab Australia, you'll access a variety of perks and benefits to support you and your family including:

6 weeks paid leave each year
14% Superannuation
Free Income Protection Insurance
Flexible working options
Access to the Saab shares matching scheme
Access to MySaab; our online store offering everything from cash back at hundreds of stores to discounts from exclusive partners

For further information about a career at Saab, visit the page adjacent:

https://www.saab.com/markets/australia/careers/saab-life

Welcome to MVSI!

We are a dynamic team of 70+ professionals, spread across the Globe with offices in Australia, EMEA, the US and the Philippines. Our innovative SaaS product is trusted by banks, Fintech’s and crypto services worldwide to manage their AML/KYC Compliance requirements. We have always harnessed the power of cutting-edge technology and are currently exploring the exciting realm of LLM (think ChatGPT) to further elevate our offerings. Our company culture is built on a foundation of support and commitment to career development. 

We truly value our people and invite you to join us to experience all that we have to offer!

Why Apply?

Get an awesome career! Ours is a specialised industry, and experience is in short supply. We employ people from all walks of life and train them to be certified professionals over time. This is a fabulous opportunity to find a new direction, build transferable skills, and enjoy a profession where every day is interesting, and you'll want to come to work.

Job Summary

Our Stack: Material UI, NodeJS, React, MongoDB.

Design, build, and maintain well-architected, production-grade systems aligned with MVSI engineering standards

Develop and maintain scalable MERN-based services with a strong focus on quality, performance, and test coverage

Improve system reliability, security, resilience, and cost efficiency across the platform

Decompose and modernise legacy or monolithic systems into scalable microservices

Collaborate with stakeholders to translate business requirements into robust technical solutions

Participate in shared operational support and contribute to production stability and incident resolution

Champion continuous improvement, engineering best practices, and automation

Learn, grow, and help raise the engineering bar across the team

About the role

We are seeking a Smart Grid / AMI Test Engineer to lead the testing and validation of next‑generation AMI technology and smart meters that will form the core metering platform.

 

This role is at the forefront of our Smart Grid transformation. You will be hands‑on with new meter hardware, communications technologies, and system platforms, ensuring they are robust, interoperable, and production‑ready before large‑scale deployment. This includes extensive hands‑on testing, configuration, experimentation, and fault analysis of physical meters and associated systems.

 

 

Key responsibilities

Lead acceptance and validation testing of new and existing AMI and Smart Grid technologies to ensure readiness for deployment and maximise business value

Develop, maintain and implement test strategies, including defining test scope, approach, estimates and acceptance criteria aligned with business, project and regulatory requirements

Design and maintain structured test plans, procedures and test cases covering functional, integration and system requirements

Implement and integrate automated testing tools into test execution workflows and system delivery pipelines to reduce manual effort and accelerate release cycles

Collaborate with engineering, operations teams and other stakeholder to embed automation into operational workflows and ensure alignment with system and business requirements

Install, configure, integrate and maintain test equipment, meters, communication devices and supporting systems to support evolving technologies

Provide technical input into deployment, commissioning, system enhancements and operational procedures (e.g., firmware updates, remote configuration)

Develop and deliver training, documentation and knowledge sharing initiatives to uplift capability across Network Operations, asset management and broader teams

 

To learn more about the responsibilities of this role, visit our Careers website and access the full position description.




 

What you’ll need from day one

Tertiary or equivalent qualifications in Information Communications Technology, Electrical Engineering, Electronics Engineering, Telecommunications, Computer Engineering, Computer Science or a related discipline

Relevant certifications or formal training in software/testing methodologies (e.g., ISTQB or equivalent) are advantageous

Experience and knowledge of electricity distribution and electricity metering

Strong understanding of AMI and Smart Grid architectures, including smart meters, communications networks (RF, cellular, PLC) and Head-End Systems (HES)

Solid knowledge of testing methodologies and lifecycle management, including test strategy, planning, execution, defect management and reporting

Strong knowledge of test automation frameworks, tools and equipment, including UFT/QTP, NI VISA, LabVIEW, Selenium and similar technologies

Good understanding of communication protocols and networking fundamentals, including Ethernet, TCP/IP, RS-232, MODBUS, GPIB and USB

Experience and knowledge of electricity distribution and electricity metering

Knowledge or experience in developing software and/or embedded applications for smart metering, AMI, or smart grid systems, including firmware-level interactions and device integration

 

If you believe you have what it takes but don’t meet every requirement—or if you’d like to discuss flexible working arrangements to meet the key responsibilities —we encourage you to get in touch.

At Podium, we bring AI Employees to local businesses that turn every conversation into revenue. Trusted by 60,000+ businesses across Auto, Home Services, and Aesthetics, Podium captures and converts leads 24/7, driving both new business and repeat customers.

In under 24 months, we crossed $100M in AI Agent ARR, scaling 300% year-over-year. During this time, we’ve deployed 10,000 AI employees to empower real business outcomes for our customers. Podium is building what we believe will be the most impactful AI employee ecosystem for local business.

Podium has been recognized as the Best AI Implementation by Inc. Magazine, highlighted by OpenAI for building revenue-driving AI Agents, and awarded the #1 AI Agent for Business Operations by G2.

Our growth is fueled by hiring exceptional people, holding them to high standards, and creating opportunities for them to grow and make an impact. Our operating principles guide daily behavior and ensure we hire people who will thrive at Podium. The Role

Podium is looking for a talented AI Engineer where your primary mandate is twofold: rebuild and modernise our lead funnel using AI-first architecture, and design and deploy a suite of internal AI agents that eliminate repetitive, manual work for our AEs and SDRs — giving them more time to sell.

If you thrive in fast-paced, highly iterative environments, enjoy solving complex distributed systems challenges, and want to see your work drive immediate business impact, this role is for you!

What you will be doing

Build and scale the AI Agent platform — enabling high-volume, real-time conversational workflows that deliver accuracy and low latency at scale.

Design and implement APIs, services, and infrastructure that power multi-turn, cross-channel customer interactions.

Own the full lifecycle: architecture, implementation, deployment, and ongoing reliability.

Prototype rapidly, iterate with live interaction data, and continuously improve system performance and user experience.

Implement observability, monitoring, and operational best practices to ensure reliability in production, focusing on agent accuracy and latency.

Collaborate with engineers, product managers, and AI/ML scientists to deliver end-to-end features that power critical business outcomes.

What you should have

5-8+ years of professional software engineering experience with at least one modern language such as Go, Python, or Elixir.

1+ years of professional experience deploying and maintaining AI agents in production environments that interact with tools, APIs, or real-world workflows.

Proven success designing and maintaining distributed or high-throughput systems.

Strong problem-solving ability and comfort navigating ambiguity in large-scale systems.

Excellent communication skills and the ability to work effectively across teams.

A willingness to work across the entire stack—from infrastructure to APIs to user-facing components—wherever the problem needs solving.

Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment with shifting priorities.

What we hope you have

1+ years of professional experience deploying and maintaining AI agents in production environments that interact with tools, APIs, or real-world workflows.

Practical experience designing and implementing evaluations for LLM behavior — including accuracy, safety, reliability, and cost monitoring.

Strong understanding of prompt design, context engineering, and guardrail strategies for dependable, interpretable agent behavior.

Experience with modern agent frameworks such as LangGraph, CrewAI, or AutoGen, including multi-step reasoning, memory, and orchestration patterns.

Hands-on experience with fine-tuning and data curation for improving model performance.

AMC Search is the Training and Consultancy arm of the Australian Maritime College.  Based in Launceston Tasmania, AMC Search serves the Australian and international maritime and defence industries. Utilising the Australian Maritime College's suite of maritime research, training and project resources, AMC Search can provide access to the largest range of facilities available in the southern hemisphere. These are supported by the personnel resources of Australia's national maritime training, education and research institute.




About the Role

We are looking for a ‘hands-on’ Autonomous Maritime Systems Engineer to support the development and delivery of AMC Search’s Autonomous Maritime Systems (AMS) capabilities, with a primary focus on engineering project design and delivery. This 12-month fixed-term role involves applying practical engineering skills and experience to the design, development, integration, and testing of autonomous maritime systems. The position also supports the execution of engineering consultancy projects, delivering high-quality, fit-for-purpose solutions to clients. 

Key Responsibilities 

Lead the design and development of autonomous maritime systems, with a primary focus on mechanical and electrical systems and end-to-end integration 
Develop system architectures and define interfaces across hardware and software components 
Integrate sensors, actuators, and communication systems into autonomous platforms (AUVs, USVs) 
Support in-house manufacture, assembly, and commissioning of prototype systems and components 
Develop and document hardware and system-level test procedures 
Execute verification and validation activities, including field testing and trials 
Analyse test data and produce clear, structured technical reports and validation documentation 
Support troubleshooting, fault finding, and continuous improvement of system performance 
Contribute to engineering consultancy projects, including concept development, analysis, and client engagement 
Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams across engineering and operations 
Support training within the Defence and Autonomous Systems function

About you 

Demonstrated self-starter with a positive, outcome focused mindset towards delivery of projects 
Practical engineering experience and strong understanding of maritime systems (both mechanical and electrical) 
Strong engineering and technical problem-solving abilities  
Strong interpersonal and communication skills, including a collaborative mindset – you naturally consult with engineers around you
Australian Citizen (as a requirement to obtain Australian Government security clearance)




Desired Skills and Experience

Qualifications

Bachelor of Engineering in a field relevant to Maritime Engineering or Robotics or proven ability to deliver engineering consultancy and project work that meets or exceeds client expectations 

Desired Experience 

Experience with autonomous maritime systems (AUVs, USVs) and related technologies 
Experience in system integration across mechanical, electrical, and software domains 
Minimum of 5 years’ experience in similar roles 
Experience with software development - ROS2, Python, or C++ 
Networking fundamentals: TCP/IP, Ethernet, serial communications 
Experience with CAN bus systems, including NMEA 2000 marine networks 
Experience conducting structural analysis using Finite Element Modelling 
Experience planning and executing field trials  
Eligibility for or already hold an Australian Government Security Vetting Agency (AGSVA) Clearance 

 

Why Join Us?

Be part of a collaborative and supportive team environment that values professional integrity and shared expertise
Access ongoing opportunities for learning and skills development
Contribute to the design, integration, testing, and delivery of autonomous maritime systems within multidisciplinary defence-focused engineering projects.

Application

If this opportunity aligns with your experience and career interests, we invite you to apply. Please submit a covering letter addressed to the Manager, Defence, Autonomous Systems, Engineering & Consultancy, outlining your interest in the role and relevant suitability, together with your current curriculum vitae and the contact details of two professional referees.

This role requires eligibility to obtain and maintain an Australian Government security clearance; therefore, Australian citizenship is required.

You are passionate about tech and engaging with and advising clients  

You likely possess an advanced degree in a quantitative field such as computer science, applied mathematics, statistics, or machine learning.  At Avanade we also support an equivalent combination of education and experience.  

Proven success in consultative/complex technical sales and deployment projects, architecture, design, implementation, and/or support of highly distributed applications required  

Extensive experience working with large complex enterprise accounts architecting cloud solutions for data estate workloads and implementing large scale data projects within the cloud environment  

You are acknowledged for driving decisions collaboratively, resolving conflicts and ensuring follow through with exceptional verbal and written communication skills. Ability to orchestrate, lead, and influence remote teams, ensuring successful implementation of customer projects  

Enterprise-scale technical experience with cloud and hybrid infrastructures, architecture designs, database migrations, and technology management
